result:
## Summary

The fix addresses the `CoroutineContext` leak via thread local in `UndispatchedCoroutine` when using a custom `ContinuationInterceptor` (not a `CoroutineDispatcher`) and the coroutine completes without suspension.

### Root Cause

When `withContext` takes "Fast Path #2" (context changes but dispatcher stays the same), it creates an `UndispatchedCoroutine`. On JVM, the `UndispatchedCoroutine` constructor's `init` block calls `saveThreadContext()`, which sets a `ThreadLocal<Pair<CoroutineContext, Any?>>` called `threadStateToRecover`. This thread local is supposed to be cleaned up in `afterResume()`, but when the coroutine completes without suspending, `afterResume()` is never called — the result is returned directly from `undispatchedResult()`. The thread local value then leaks, holding a reference to the `CoroutineContext`.

### Changes Made

Three files were modified:

1. **`kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/internal/Scopes.kt`** — Added an open method `afterCompletionUndispatched()` to `ScopeCoroutine` (no-op by default). This is the hook for cleanup when a coroutine completes synchronously without suspension.

2. **`kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/intrinsics/Undispatched.kt`** — Added a call to `afterCompletionUndispatched()` in `undispatchedResult()` after determining that the coroutine completed without suspension (after paths 1 and 2 — suspension — are excluded).

3. **`kotlinx-coroutines-core/jvm/src/CoroutineContext.kt`** — Overrode `afterCompletionUndispatched()` in `UndispatchedCoroutine` to call `threadStateToRecover.remove()`, cleaning up the thread local that was set in the `init` block.
